title: The Deeper Meaning of the Iran Nuclear Talks: ISIS, the Middle East and Beyond
summary: "ISIS cannot be defeated with airstrikes, and that's all the West seems prepared to do. The coalition needs local and regional support. It must be prepared to send in large numbers of ground forces for a long time. Only Iran will be both able and willing to do that."
body: It's in Washington's interest to separate the nuclear talks from other regional crises; it's not in Iran's. But that's only as far as the nuclear negotiations are concerned. The region is disintegrating and even the merest coordination with Iran would make a difference. Hardliners on both sides worry that their respective governments will agree to a " bad deal " to get the nuclear issue out of the way and focus on ISIS . That's because any meaningful engagement with the other side is predicated on resolving the nuclear issue first....
